statement.focus_question: Let\[f(n) = \sum_{p<n}\frac{1}{n-p}.\]Is it true that\[\liminf f(n)=1\]and\[\limsup f(n)=\infty?
statement.normalized: Let\[f(n) = \sum_{p<n}\frac{1}{n-p}.\]Is it true that\[\liminf f(n)=1\]and\[\limsup f(n)=\infty?\]Is it true that $f(n)=o(\log\log n)$ for all $n$?
statement.original: Let\[f(n) = \sum_{p<n}\frac{1}{n-p}.\]Is it true that\[\liminf f(n)=1\]and\[\limsup f(n)=\infty?\]Is it true that $f(n)=o(\log\log n)$ for all $n$?
